raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a causal or expressive link between mental/spiritual states and physical conditions to guide behavior or interpretation of illness",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "thoughts possess independent ontological status capable of influencing matter",
    "physical illness can originate from or be shaped by non-physical mental contents",
    "a lawful correspondence exists between the quality of thoughts and bodily states"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent within a mind-first or psychophysical parallelism ontology, but rests on unstated premises about the primacy and causal efficacy of thought",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "a verifiable or pragmatically effective psychosomatic mechanism exists, or belief in the claim reliably produces beneficial changes in attention, emotion regulation, or health-related behavior",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "biological causation is primary and the claim encourages attribution of illness solely to 'thought quality,' fostering guilt, delayed medical treatment, or neglect of material factors"
}
